rationale: The primary home at this Saratoga Springs, UT address displays moderate shadow casting from its eave lines — shadows are clearly defined but not elongated enough to indicate a steep 8:12+ pitch. The ridge-to-eave geometry shows the ridge set well inward from the building perimeter, consistent with a mid-range slope. Multiple hip and gable roof planes are distinctly visible from satellite, suggesting meaningful vertical rise without being dramatically steep. The complex hip/gable combination roof style and northern Utah climate (snow load requirements) both support a 6:12 dominant pitch, which is the most common standard in Wasatch Front suburban construction. Confidence is moderate as image contrast and tree shadows partially obscure some eave edges.
